Q01 search for exact-phrase 'Painlevé III hierarchy' returned 4 arXiv hits; web-search supplement added 2 more named higher-order-PIII papers; 5 distinct PIII-hierarchy constructions identified in literature (Sakka, Atkin Lenard-recursion, Atkin-Claeys-Mezzadri matrix-model, Bilman-Ling-Miller NLS rogue waves, Wang-Xu 2025 isomonodromic). Slot 181 NULL signal does not hold under direct exact-phrase search.

Entry 4 cited as 'Joshi-Lustri-Topp 2014' at arXiv:1403.1235 is a hallucinated identifier (post-031 bibliographic-pre-verification rule failure mode). The arXiv ID 1403.1235 resolves to 'Its-Lisovyy-Tykhyy 2014, Connection problem for the sine-Gordon/Painlevé III tau function and irregular conformal blocks' (Int. Math. Res. Notices 2015, 8903-8924). No published Joshi-Lustri-Topp 2014 paper appears to exist; closest is Joshi-Lustri 2015 arXiv:1503.01302 on discrete PI Stokes phenomena (no Topp co-author; not PIII).
method: arxiv.org/abs/1403.1235 citation_author meta-tag inspection + web_search for Joshi-Lustri-Topp 2014

claim: Entry 1 anchor (Mazzocco-Mo 2006, arXiv:nlin/0610066) verified PASS: authors=Mazzocco-Marta + Mo-Man-Yue; title='The Hamiltonian Structure of the Second Painlevé Hierarchy'; date 2006-10-27; DOI 10.1088/0951-7715/20/12/006 (Nonlinearity 20, 2007). However the paper is about PII hierarchy (NOT PIII), used as a methodological-template anchor for Frontier-A's intended PIII(D6) hierarchy construction.

Entry 2 anchor (Bobrova-Mazzocco 2020, arXiv:2012.11010) verified PASS: authors=Bobrova-Irina + Mazzocco-Marta; title='The Sigma Form for the PII Hierarchy'; date 2020-12-20; DOI 10.1016/j.geomphys.2021.104271 (J. Geom. Phys. 170, 2021). Like Entry 1, this is a PII hierarchy paper used as methodological-template anchor.

Entries 3, 5, 6, 7 verified PASS via arxiv citation_author meta-tag inspection: (3) Joshi-N + Mazzocco-M, math/0212117, 'Existence and Uniqueness of Tri-tronquée Solutions of the second Painlevé hierarchy', 2002; (5) Iorgov + Iwaki + Lisovyy + Zhuravlov, 2505.16803, 'Many-faced Painlevé I', 2025 (IILZ = these 4 authors); (6) Lenells + Roussillon, 2407.03464, 'Semiclassical limit of a non-polynomial q-Askey scheme', 2024 (LR = these 2 authors, PIII3 = PIII(D6) degeneration); (7) Its + Lisovyy + Prokhorov, 1604.03082, 'Monodromy dependence and connection formulae', 2016 (ILP = these 3 authors).
method: arxiv.org/abs/* raw HTML citation_author meta-tag inspection

claim: Entry 8 (Sakai 2001 foundational reference) identified as: Hidetaka Sakai, 'Rational surfaces associated with affine root systems and geometry of the Painlevé equations', Communications in Mathematical Physics 220, no. 1 (2001), 165-229, DOI 10.1007/PL00005590. This is the foundational paper introducing the PIII(D6)/D7/D8 Sakai-surface-type classification.
method: web_search query 'Sakai 2001 Rational surfaces Painleve Comm Math Phys DOI'
